meta.theme: 실생활 영어 표현 집중 배치 + 일상회화 테마팩

english_theme_packs.theme_title: 일상과 직장 사이에서 쓰는 진짜 영어

expressions.expression: I'm swamped right now — can we catch up later?
expressions.meaning: 지금 정말 바빠서요 — 나중에 얘기할 수 있을까요?
expressions.nuance: 'Swamped'는 늪(swamp)에 빠진 것처럼 일에 파묻혀 있다는 뜻입니다. 'Busy'보다 훨씬 생생하고 자연스러운 표현으로, 원어민이 일상에서 가장 자주 쓰는 단어 중 하나입니다. 'Catch up'은 오랜만에 또는 잠깐 만나서 이야기 나누는 것을 뜻합니다. '지금 당장은 어렵지만 대화를 피하는 게 아니라 나중에 꼭 하겠다'는 뜻을 자연스럽게 전달합니다.
expressions.dialogue:
A: Hey, do you have a minute? I wanted to run something by you.
B: I'm swamped right now — we have a client call in ten minutes and I'm still prepping. Can we catch up after lunch?
A: Of course, no rush. I'll ping you around 1:30?
B: Perfect. See you then.
expressions.example: I'm completely swamped this week with the product launch — can we push the review meeting to Monday?
expressions.practice: I'm a bit swamped at the moment. Can I get back to you by end of day?

expressions.expression: No worries at all — these things happen.
expressions.meaning: 전혀 괜찮아요 — 그럴 수 있죠. (사과를 받아줄 때 쓰는 가장 자연스러운 표현)
expressions.nuance: 'It's okay'나 'Don't worry'보다 훨씬 자연스럽고 따뜻한 표현입니다. 'No worries'는 호주에서 왔지만 지금은 미국·영국에서도 일상적으로 씁니다. 'These things happen'을 덧붙이면 '이런 일은 누구에게나 생긴다'는 공감의 뉘앙스가 추가됩니다. 상대방의 사과를 받아줄 때 진심 어린 관대함을 보여줍니다.
expressions.dialogue:
A: I'm so sorry I forgot to send you the file before the meeting. That must have been really inconvenient.
B: No worries at all — these things happen. I had a copy saved from last week, so we were fine.
A: Still, I should have been more careful.
B: Honestly, don't stress about it. Just send it over when you have a chance.
expressions.example: No worries at all — these things happen. Let's just focus on moving forward from here.
expressions.practice: No worries! These things happen. Just send it over when you get a chance.

expressions.expression: That totally slipped my mind. My bad!
expressions.meaning: 완전히 깜빡했어요. 제 실수예요! (잊었을 때 자연스럽게 사과하는 표현)
expressions.nuance: 'Slipped my mind'는 기억에서 빠져나가 버렸다는 생생한 표현입니다. 'I forgot'보다 훨씬 구어적이고 자연스럽습니다. 'My bad'는 'My mistake' 또는 'Sorry about that'의 캐주얼한 버전으로, 젊은 직장인들이 가장 많이 쓰는 사과 표현 중 하나입니다. 지나치게 격식 없이 쓰면 진지함이 부족하게 들릴 수 있으니 상황을 파악해서 쓰세요.
expressions.dialogue:
A: Did you end up booking the conference room for tomorrow?
B: Oh no — that completely slipped my mind. My bad! I'll do it right now. Is 2pm still okay?
A: Actually, we need it at 3pm now. Can you check if that's available?
B: On it. I'll confirm within five minutes.
expressions.example: The meeting reminder — that totally slipped my mind. My bad! I should have set a calendar alert.
expressions.practice: Oh, sending you the summary — that slipped my mind completely. My bad, I'll have it to you within the hour.

expressions.expression: Let me sleep on it and get back to you tomorrow.
expressions.meaning: 좀 더 생각해보고 내일 답드릴게요. (즉답을 피하고 시간을 요청할 때)
expressions.nuance: 'Sleep on it'은 밤새 생각한다는 의미로, 중요한 결정을 급하게 하지 않겠다는 신중함을 표현합니다. 즉답을 강요받는 상황에서 '거절하는 것도 아니고 수락하는 것도 아닌' 상태를 유지하며 시간을 벌 때 매우 유용합니다. 거절처럼 들리지 않으면서도 'get back to you tomorrow'로 책임감 있는 후속 조치를 약속합니다.
expressions.dialogue:
A: So, are you interested in taking on the regional manager role? We'd love your answer by this week.
B: I'm genuinely honored, and I'm very interested. But I want to make sure I'm making the right decision for everyone involved. Can I sleep on it and get back to you tomorrow morning?
A: Of course. Take the time you need.
B: I appreciate that. I'll have a clear answer for you by 9am.
expressions.example: That's a significant proposal — let me sleep on it and get back to you with a proper response tomorrow.
expressions.practice: I don't want to give you a half-considered answer. Let me sleep on it and get back to you tomorrow.

expressions.expression: I'm all ears — what's on your mind?
expressions.meaning: 잘 듣고 있어요 — 무슨 생각이세요? (적극적으로 상대방의 말을 듣겠다는 표현)
expressions.nuance: 'All ears'는 귀가 전부라는 재미있는 표현으로, 온전히 당신의 말에 집중하겠다는 뜻입니다. 'What's on your mind?'는 '무슨 생각을 하고 있어요?' 또는 '무슨 말이 하고 싶어요?'라는 뜻으로, 상대가 망설이거나 뭔가 말하고 싶어하는 것 같을 때 열어주는 표현입니다. 리더십, 팀원 관계, 1대1 미팅에서 심리적 안전감을 만들어주는 표현입니다.
expressions.dialogue:
Team member: Do you have a second? I've been meaning to talk to you about something.
Manager: Of course — I'm all ears. What's on your mind?
Team member: It's about the new process. I have a few concerns I wasn't sure how to raise.
Manager: I'm glad you came to me. Tell me everything.
expressions.example: I've set aside the next 30 minutes — I'm all ears. What's on your mind about the project?
expressions.practice: I'm all ears. You've seemed a bit distracted lately — what's on your mind?

master_paragraph.text: I know you're swamped right now, so no worries at all if the timing's bad — these things happen. Just a heads up: I realized something totally slipped my mind from our last conversation, and my bad for not flagging it sooner. I don't want to give you a rushed answer on the bigger question, so let me sleep on it and get back to you tomorrow with something concrete. But if you have a minute at some point today, I'm all ears — what's on your mind about how things are going?
master_paragraph.translation: 지금 엄청 바쁘신 거 알아요, 그러니 타이밍이 안 좋으면 전혀 괜찮아요 — 그럴 수 있죠. 미리 말씀드리자면: 지난 대화에서 뭔가 완전히 깜빡했다는 걸 깨달았어요, 더 일찍 말씀드리지 못해서 제 실수예요. 더 큰 질문에 대해 급하게 답하고 싶지 않아서요, 좀 더 생각해보고 내일 구체적으로 답드릴게요. 하지만 오늘 중에 잠깐 시간이 되시면, 잘 듣고 있을게요 — 요즘 어떻게 돌아가고 있는지 무슨 생각이세요?

english_expressions.meaning: 엄청 바쁘다, 일에 파묻혀 있다.
english_expressions.example: I'm swamped with reports this week — can we reschedule for next Monday?
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Are you free for a quick chat?
B: I'm completely swamped right now — back-to-back meetings until 5. Can I ping you after?
A: Sure, no rush.
B: Thanks, I'll reach out around 5:30.

english_expressions.expression: Can you do me a favor?
english_expressions.meaning: 부탁 하나 들어줄 수 있어요? (부탁을 꺼내기 전에 먼저 허락을 구하는 표현)
english_expressions.example: Can you do me a favor and forward that email thread to the team? I don't have everyone's addresses.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Can you do me a favor?
B: Depends on what it is — just kidding. Sure, what's up?
A: Can you cover the 10am standup for me tomorrow? I have a dentist appointment.
B: No problem, I'll be there.

english_expressions.expression: Let's grab coffee / Let's grab lunch
english_expressions.meaning: 커피 한잔 해요 / 점심 같이 해요. 가볍게 만남을 제안하는 표현.
english_expressions.example: It's been a while — let's grab coffee sometime this week and catch up properly.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Hey, I've been wanting to pick your brain about the new marketing direction.
B: I'd love that. Let's grab lunch Friday — my treat.
A: That works perfectly. Thai place on 3rd?
B: You read my mind.

english_expressions.expression: I'll get back to you on that
english_expressions.meaning: 그 부분은 확인 후 다시 연락드리겠습니다. 즉답 대신 후속 조치를 약속할 때.
english_expressions.example: I don't have the exact numbers with me right now — let me pull the data and I'll get back to you on that by end of day.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Do you know what the Q2 retention rate was for the enterprise segment specifically?
B: Hmm, I know the overall number but not the segment breakdown off the top of my head. I'll get back to you on that — can I send you an email after lunch?
A: Perfect, that works.
B: Give me until 2pm.

english_expressions.meaning: 처음 듣는 얘기네요, 저는 몰랐어요. (놀람과 함께 처음 알았다는 것을 표현)
english_expressions.example: The policy changed last week? That's news to me — I must have missed the announcement.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Did you hear? They're moving the whole team to the new office next month.
B: What? That's completely news to me. When was that decided?
A: Apparently the email went out Tuesday.
B: I never got it. Let me check my spam folder.

english_expressions.meaning: 제가 처리하고 있습니다, 바로 할게요. (즉각 행동에 나섰다는 것을 짧게 확인시켜주는 표현)
english_expressions.example:
A: The server's throwing errors on the checkout page.
B: I'm on it.
english_expressions.dialogue:
Manager: The client just called — they need the revised proposal before 4pm.
You: I'm on it. I just need to update two sections and do a final proofread.
Manager: How long?
You: An hour max. You'll have it by 3.

english_expressions.expression: Sorry, I missed that — could you repeat?
english_expressions.meaning: 죄송한데 못 들었어요 — 다시 말씀해주실 수 있어요? 화상회의·전화에서 필수 표현.
english_expressions.example: Sorry, I missed that last part — there was a bit of background noise on my end. Could you repeat what you said about the timeline?
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: ...and so I think the best approach is to—
B: Sorry, I missed that — you broke up for a second. Could you say that last part again?
A: Of course. I was saying the best approach is to launch in phases rather than all at once.
B: Got it, that makes sense.

english_expressions.meaning: 괜찮아요, 걱정 마세요. 사과를 받아줄 때 가장 자연스럽고 따뜻한 표현.
english_expressions.example:
A: I'm so sorry I'm five minutes late!
B: No worries at all — we literally just sat down.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: I accidentally deleted the wrong version of the file. I'm really sorry.
B: No worries — I have it backed up in the shared drive. Want me to send the link?
A: Yes please, that would be amazing.
B: On it.

english_expressions.meaning: 깜빡했어요, 기억에서 빠져나가 버렸어요.
english_expressions.example: I meant to bring those documents — it completely slipped my mind this morning. Can I email them instead?
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Did you end up calling the vendor about the delivery date?
B: Oh no — that completely slipped my mind. I'll call them right now.
A: Please do — we need confirmation before tomorrow.
B: I'm dialing as we speak. I'll have an answer in ten minutes.

english_expressions.meaning: 이해해요, 공감해요. 상대방의 감정이나 상황에 진심으로 공감할 때.
english_expressions.example:
A: This project has been exhausting — I haven't left before 9pm all week.
B: I feel you. Last quarter I was in the same situation.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: I keep trying to push back on the unrealistic deadline but nobody listens.
B: I feel you. I went through the exact same thing with the Q1 launch. Did you try putting the trade-offs in writing?
A: Not yet.
B: Do that. When the risks are documented, people pay more attention.

english_expressions.meaning: 간단히 말하자면, 요약하자면. 긴 배경 설명을 생략하고 핵심만 말할 때.
english_expressions.example: Long story short — the merger fell through, we're pivoting back to the original plan, and the timeline shifts by six weeks.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: What happened with the client situation? I heard it got complicated.
B: Oh, it was a whole thing. Long story short — there was a miscommunication about deliverables, the client thought they were getting a custom build, we thought it was off the shelf. We got it sorted, but it took three extra calls.
A: Oof. How'd they take it?
B: Better than expected, honestly.

english_expressions.meaning: 잘 이해가 안 되는데요. 이해하지 못했을 때 정중하게 설명을 요청하는 표현.
english_expressions.example: I'm not sure I follow — can you walk me through the logic one more time?
english_expressions.dialogue:
Presenter: So if we apply the regression model to the cohort data, and factor in the seasonality adjustment, we should see a natural normalization by Q3.
You: I'm not sure I follow the last part — what does 'natural normalization' look like in practice? Can you show that on the chart?
Presenter: Great question. So on this slide...

english_expressions.meaning: 완전히 이해돼요, 맞는 말이에요. 상대방의 설명이나 제안에 진심으로 동의할 때.
english_expressions.example: Oh, you're launching in the US before Korea because of the time zone overlap with your team — that makes total sense.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: The reason we're not discounting anymore is that our price anchors customer perception of quality. Once we discount, it's almost impossible to reset.
B: That makes total sense. It's like how luxury brands never go on sale.
A: Exactly. Once you're in the discounting loop, you're competing on price, and that's not a game we can win.

english_expressions.meaning: 완벽하게 해냈어요, 딱 맞혀요. 잘 해냈거나 정확히 맞췄을 때 쓰는 칭찬.
english_expressions.example: That presentation was exactly what the client needed — you really nailed it today.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: How did the pitch go? I was nervous the whole time.
B: You nailed it. The part where you connected their specific pain point to our solution — I could see their body language change completely.
A: Really? I thought I stumbled on the pricing slide.
B: Nobody noticed. All they could talk about afterward was the ROI projection.

english_expressions.meaning: 잠깐만 기다려 주세요, 조금만 참아주세요. 뭔가 찾거나 준비하는 동안 양해를 구할 때.
english_expressions.example: Bear with me for a second — I need to pull up the right slide.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Bear with me while I find the right document — I have too many tabs open.
B: Take your time.
A: Sorry about this... Okay, here it is. So this is the data I was talking about.
B: No rush. Thanks for digging that up.

english_expressions.meaning: 저는 괜찮아요, 그렇게 합시다. 제안이나 시간/방법에 동의할 때.
english_expressions.example:
A: How about we meet Thursday at 3pm?
B: That works for me.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: So the plan is to do a soft launch in two markets, gather data for six weeks, then decide on full rollout. Does that work for everyone?
B: That works for me — six weeks feels like enough to see real trends.
C: Same here.
A: Great, let's lock that in.

english_expressions.meaning: 뭔가 숨겨진 조건이 있는 거 아니에요? 너무 좋아 보이는 제안에 의심할 때.
english_expressions.example: A new client offering triple our usual rate with a fast turnaround? What's the catch?
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: They're offering us exclusive access to their platform, unlimited API calls, and a dedicated support team — for free.
B: That sounds amazing, but what's the catch?
A: They want our user data to train their model.
B: There it is. That changes things significantly.

english_expressions.expression: Off the top of my head
english_expressions.meaning: 즉석에서 생각해보면, 바로 떠오르는 건. 정확하지 않을 수 있음을 인정하면서 바로 답할 때.
english_expressions.example: Off the top of my head, I'd say the project is about 70% done — but let me check the actual tracker before we put that in the report.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Do you know how many active users we had last month?
B: Off the top of my head, somewhere around 48,000 — but don't quote me on that. Let me pull the exact number from the dashboard.
A: Ballpark is fine for now, I just need a rough sense.
B: Then roughly 50k, give or take.

english_expressions.meaning: 한번 해봐요, 시도해봐요. 무언가를 시도해볼 것을 권유할 때.
english_expressions.example: You've never done public speaking before? Give it a shot — the first one is always scary, but you learn fast.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: I've never written a cold outreach email before. I'm not sure I'll be any good at it.
B: Just give it a shot. Write three versions, send them to a small test group, see what gets replies. You'll learn more from 10 real responses than from studying the theory.
A: That's actually a good point.
B: Start today. Even a rough draft is better than none.

english_expressions.meaning: 저 할게요, 찬성이에요, 할 의향이 있어요.
english_expressions.example: Happy hour after the Friday standup? I'm up for it — I could use a break.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: We're thinking of doing a team offsite in Busan next quarter. Would you be up for it?
B: Absolutely — I'm up for it. I think it would do the team a lot of good to spend some time outside the office.
A: Great. Any preferences on format?
B: Maybe a half-day workshop and then some downtime? I find the bonding happens more naturally when it's not all structured activities.

english_expressions.meaning: 좋은 지적이에요, 그거 중요한 포인트네요. 상대방의 의견이나 지적에 진심으로 동의할 때.
english_expressions.example: That's a good point about the security implications — I hadn't thought about it from that angle.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: I think we should be careful about how we frame the data. If we cherry-pick these three months, it looks great, but the full-year picture tells a different story.
B: That's a genuinely good point. We need to present the full picture — especially to stakeholders who might ask follow-up questions.
A: Exactly. Partial data now creates credibility problems later.
B: Agreed. Let's use the full year and add context for the dip.

english_expressions.meaning: 무슨 말씀인지 이해해요, 그 뜻을 알겠어요.
english_expressions.example: I see what you mean about the user flow — the third step does feel like an unnecessary extra click.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: The problem isn't the features themselves, it's that we keep adding things without removing anything. Users feel overwhelmed.
B: I see what you mean. It's a classic addition bias — it's always easier to add than to take away.
A: Exactly. And every PM thinks their feature is essential.
B: So we need a framework for deciding what comes out before we even discuss what goes in.

english_expressions.meaning: 어떻게 생각하세요? 제안이나 아이디어에 대한 상대방의 의견을 구할 때.
english_expressions.example: I'll handle the client relationship side, you focus on the technical specs, and we present together on Thursday. How does that sound?
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: Here's what I'm thinking — I'll draft the proposal by Wednesday, you review and add the financial projections, and we send it Thursday morning. How does that sound?
B: That works. Wednesday is fine for me. Are you okay with me restructuring the executive summary if I feel it needs it?
A: Absolutely — that section needs your touch.
B: Great. Let's do it.

english_expressions.meaning: 그건 제 실수예요, 제 책임이에요. 책임을 회피하지 않고 직접적으로 인정할 때.
english_expressions.example: The miscommunication with the client — that's on me. I should have confirmed in writing after the call.
english_expressions.dialogue:
Manager: The deliverable was late and the client had to follow up twice.
You: That's completely on me. I underestimated how long the data cleaning would take and I didn't flag the delay early enough. It won't happen again.
Manager: I appreciate you owning it. What's the plan going forward?
You: I've already added buffer time to all my estimates this quarter, and I'll escalate the moment I see a timeline at risk.

english_expressions.meaning: 우리 괜찮아요, 문제없어요. 오해나 갈등이 해소됐거나 상황이 괜찮다는 것을 확인할 때.
english_expressions.example:
A: I just wanted to check — are we good after yesterday's disagreement?
B: We're good. I actually appreciated the direct feedback.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: I was a bit blunt in the meeting earlier. I hope that didn't come across the wrong way.
B: We're good — I actually think the meeting needed that kind of directness. It got us to a decision faster.
A: I'm glad it landed okay. I was second-guessing myself afterward.
B: Don't. It was the right call.

english_expressions.meaning: 좀 더 생각해볼게요. 중요한 결정을 즉각 내리지 않고 시간을 갖고 싶을 때.
english_expressions.example: It's a solid offer — let me sleep on it and give you a final answer tomorrow morning.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: So we want to know: are you in or out for the new venture?
B: I'm genuinely intrigued. But it's a big commitment and I want to give you a real answer, not a rushed one. Can I sleep on it and call you tomorrow?
A: Of course. We're not closing the round until end of month.
B: I'll have a clear yes or no by 9am tomorrow.

english_expressions.meaning: 솔직히 말하면, 사실대로 말하자면. 직접적인 의견을 말하기 전에 쓰는 표현.
english_expressions.example: To be honest, I think the timeline is too aggressive — I'd feel more comfortable with two extra weeks.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: What did you think of the new branding direction?
B: To be honest? I think it's safe. It's polished, but it doesn't feel distinct enough to stand out in our category.
A: That's pretty much what I've been thinking too but wasn't sure how to say.
B: I figured someone had to say it. Let's bring it up in the next review.

english_expressions.meaning: 나쁘지 않아요, 그럭저럭 잘 지내고 있어요. 근황을 물어볼 때 적당히 좋다고 답하는 표현.
english_expressions.example:
A: How's the new role going so far?
B: Honestly, I can't complain — the team is great and the work is interesting.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: How's everything going with the new team?
B: I can't complain, honestly. The onboarding was a bit intense but everyone's been really welcoming, and I'm already getting to work on interesting problems.
A: That's great to hear.
B: I think I made the right call switching over.

english_expressions.meaning: 솔직히 말할게요, 사실을 말하자면. 다소 솔직한 말을 꺼낼 때 앞에 붙이는 표현.
english_expressions.example: Not gonna lie — that product demo was one of the best I've seen this year.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: How was the conference overall?
B: Not gonna lie, the morning keynote was a bit of a letdown — a lot of hype, not much substance. But the afternoon breakout sessions were genuinely useful.
A: Yeah, I heard the same thing from a few people.
B: The networking was worth it alone though.

english_expressions.meaning: 그러게 말이에요, 저도 그렇게 생각해요. 상대방의 말에 강하게 공감하고 동의할 때.
english_expressions.example:
A: This commute is absolutely brutal in the rain.
B: You're telling me — I was stuck for 40 minutes.
english_expressions.dialogue:
A: The approval process for simple decisions here takes forever. Three layers of sign-off for a 200-dollar purchase?
B: You're telling me. I submitted a request two weeks ago and it's still sitting with someone.
A: Something's got to change.
B: I've been thinking about writing up a proposal for a streamlined system. Want to co-sign it?
